title: The notification that gave up after a minute
excerpt: Debugging a notification that stopped arriving after a minute: four healthy-looking layers, two quiet failures, one browser timeout.
content_html:
July 17, 2026

PopsicleBoat grew browser push notifications recently. The pitch is simple: hear when someone answers you, without handing anyone an email address. Your browser asks permission, a subscription goes to the server, and nothing personal leaves the device. I shipped it, watched the push service accept my first delivery with a tidy 201 Created, and felt good about the whole thing.

Then I replied to one of my own posts from my phone, looked at my laptop, and saw nothing.

No banner. No chime. The reply was on the site, the inbox badge lit up, the notification email arrived — every channel working except the one I’d just built. And here’s what made it a good puzzle: every layer of the push stack, inspected on its own, reported success.

The server sent the notification. The push service accepted it. The subscription was in the database. The service worker was registered. Four green lights, zero banners.

Web push is a relay race with four runners: your server signs a message and hands it to a push service (Google’s, for Chromium browsers), the push service holds it for the browser, the browser wakes a service worker, and the service worker asks the operating system to draw a banner. A dropped baton anywhere shows up the same way — silence at the finish line — and no runner files a report.

The first dropped baton was mine to find in the browser console. Notification.permission said "default". Not "granted", not "denied" — never asked. Somewhere between enabling notifications and testing them, the site’s permission had ended up back at square one — a site-data sweep, probably — while the subscription it had authorized lived on in my database, perfectly valid, pointing at a browser that would no longer show anything. In that state, a page calling new Notification() doesn’t error. It does nothing, silently, which is a bold choice for an API whose entire job is being noticed.

One re-grant later (“forever,” this time), banners worked. Victory lasted about an hour, until I noticed pushes still vanished whenever the browser wasn’t running.

That was the second baton, and it was hiding in a default I’d never questioned. Push services will happily hold a message for a browser that’s closed — that’s the whole point of the relay — but only as long as the message’s time-to-live allows. The library I use sets that TTL to sixty seconds unless told otherwise. Sixty seconds. Close your laptop, get a reply two minutes later, and the push service shrugs and discards it. The 201 it returned was entirely honest: message accepted. Nobody promised kept.

The fix is one option:

send_notification(payload, message, ttl: 86_400)

A day. That’s roughly how long “someone answered you” stays worth interrupting someone for; any older and it’s the inbox’s job, which never forgets.

Two lessons sailed home with this one. First: in a layered system where every layer answers “OK,” the bug lives in the gaps between the layers — the permission that reverted between grant and use, the message that expired between accepted and delivered. Debugging meant walking the relay in order and asking each runner not “did you succeed?” but “what did you hand the next runner, and did anyone catch it?”

Second: defaults are decisions someone else made about your product. A conservative browser permission model, a sixty-second TTL — both defensible choices, by people who’d never seen my notification settings page and its promise to hear when someone answers you. Keeping that promise meant finding every default standing between the reply and the banner, and overruling the ones that disagreed with it.

The banners arrive now. Even the morning after.

Discuss this post aboard Popsicle Boat →

sentences.en: PopsicleBoat grew browser push notifications recently.
sentences.zh: PopsicleBoat 最近增加了浏览器推送通知。

sentences.en: The pitch is simple: hear when someone answers you, without handing anyone an email address.
sentences.zh: 宣传很简单：听到有人回答你，而不需要向任何人提供电子邮件地址。

sentences.en: Your browser asks permission, a subscription goes to the server, and nothing personal leaves the device.
sentences.zh: 您的浏览器会请求许可，订阅会发送到服务器，并且不会有任何个人信息离开设备。

sentences.en: I shipped it, watched the push service accept my first delivery with a tidy 201 Created, and felt good about the whole thing.
sentences.zh: 我发货了，看着推送服务接受了我的第一次交付，并收到了整洁的 201 Created，整个事情感觉很好。

sentences.en: Four green lights, zero banners.

sentences.en: Not "granted", not "denied" — never asked.
sentences.zh: 不是“授予”，不是“拒绝”——从来没有问过。

sentences.en: Somewhere between enabling notifications and testing them, the site’s permission had ended up back at square one — a site-data sweep, probably — while the subscription it had authorized lived on in my database, perfectly valid, pointing at a browser that would no longer show anything.

sentences.zh: 在这种状态下，调用 newNotification() 的页面不会出错。

sentences.en: It does nothing, silently, which is a bold choice for an API whose entire job is being noticed.
sentences.zh: 它什么都不做，默默地进行，对于整个工作都受到关注的 API 来说，这是一个大胆的选择。

sentences.zh: 经过一次重新授予（这次是“永远”），横幅发挥了作用。

sentences.en: Victory lasted about an hour, until I noticed pushes still vanished whenever the browser wasn’t running.
sentences.zh: 胜利持续了大约一个小时，直到我注意到只要浏览器不运行，推送仍然消失。

blocks.html_en: That was the second baton, and it was hiding in a default I’d never questioned. The 201 it returned was entirely honest: message accepted. Nobody promised kept.

sentences.en: That was the second baton, and it was hiding in a default I’d never questioned.
sentences.zh: 那是第二根接力棒，它隐藏在我从未质疑过的默认状态中。

sentences.en: Push services will happily hold a message for a browser that’s closed — that’s the whole point of the relay — but only as long as the message’s time-to-live allows.
sentences.zh: 推送服务很乐意为关闭的浏览器保留一条消息——这就是中继的全部意义——但前提是消息的生存时间允许。

sentences.en: The library I use sets that TTL to sixty seconds unless told otherwise.
sentences.zh: 除非另有说明，我使用的库将 TTL 设置为六十秒。

sentences.en: Sixty seconds.
sentences.zh: 六十秒。

sentences.en: Close your laptop, get a reply two minutes later, and the push service shrugs and discards it.
sentences.zh: 关闭你的笔记本电脑，两分钟后收到回复，推送服务耸耸肩并丢弃它。

sentences.en: The 201 it returned was entirely honest: message accepted.
sentences.zh: 它返回的 201 是完全诚实的：消息已接受。

sentences.en: Nobody promised kept.
sentences.zh: 没有人答应遵守。

sentences.en: The fix is one option:
sentences.zh: 修复方法是一种选择：

blocks.html_en: A day. That’s roughly how long “someone answered you” stays worth interrupting someone for; any older and it’s the inbox’s job, which never forgets.

sentences.en: A day.
sentences.zh: 一天。

sentences.en: That’s roughly how long “someone answered you” stays worth interrupting someone for; any older and it’s the inbox’s job, which never forgets.
sentences.zh: 这大致就是“有人回答你”值得打断某人的时间；再老一点，收件箱的工作就永远不会忘记。

blocks.html_en: Two lessons sailed home with this one. First: in a layered system where every layer answers “OK,” the bug lives in the gaps between the layers — the permission that reverted between grant and use, the message that expired between accepted and delivered. Debugging meant walking the relay in order and asking each runner not “did you succeed?” but “what did you hand the next runner, and did anyone catch it?”

sentences.en: Two lessons sailed home with this one.
sentences.zh: 两堂课和这堂课一起回家了。

sentences.en: First: in a layered system where every layer answers “OK,” the bug lives in the gaps between the layers — the permission that reverted between grant and use, the message that expired between accepted and delivered.
sentences.zh: 首先：在分层系统中，每一层都回答“OK”，错误存在于各层之间的间隙中——在授予和使用之间恢复的权限，在接受和交付之间过期的消息。

sentences.en: Debugging meant walking the relay in order and asking each runner not “did you succeed?”
sentences.zh: 调试意味着按顺序走接力赛并询问每个跑步者“你成功了吗？”

sentences.en: but “what did you hand the next runner, and did anyone catch it?”
sentences.zh: 但是“你给了下一个跑步者什么，有人接住了吗？”

blocks.html_en: Second: defaults are decisions someone else made about your product. A conservative browser permission model, a sixty-second TTL — both defensible choices, by people who’d never seen my notification settings page and its promise to hear when someone answers you. Keeping that promise meant finding every default standing between the reply and the banner, and overruling the ones that disagreed with it.

sentences.en: Second: defaults are decisions someone else made about your product.
sentences.zh: 第二：默认是其他人对你的产品做出的决定。

sentences.en: A conservative browser permission model, a sixty-second TTL — both defensible choices, by people who’d never seen my notification settings page and its promise to hear when someone answers you.
sentences.zh: 保守的浏览器权限模型、六十秒的 TTL——对于那些从未见过我的通知设置页面以及它承诺在有人回答你时听到的人来说，这都是合理的选择。

sentences.en: Keeping that promise meant finding every default standing between the reply and the banner, and overruling the ones that disagreed with it.
sentences.zh: 遵守这一承诺意味着找到回复和横幅之间的所有默认设置，并否决那些不同意的设置。

blocks.html_en: The banners arrive now. Even the morning after.

sentences.en: The banners arrive now.
sentences.zh: 横幅现在到了。

sentences.en: Even the morning after.
sentences.zh: 即使是第二天早上。

content_html_zh:
July 17, 2026

PopsicleBoat 最近增加了浏览器推送通知。 宣传很简单：听到有人回答你，而不需要向任何人提供电子邮件地址。 您的浏览器会请求许可，订阅会发送到服务器，并且不会有任何个人信息离开设备。 我发货了，看着推送服务接受了我的第一次交付，并收到了整洁的 201 Created，整个事情感觉很好。

然后我用手机回复了自己的一篇帖子，查看了我的笔记本电脑，但什么也没有看到。

没有横幅。 没有铃声。 回复在网站上，收件箱徽章亮起，通知电子邮件到达——除了我刚刚建立的那个之外，每个渠道都在工作。 这就是它成为一个很好的谜题的原因：推送堆栈的每一层，单独检查，都报告成功。

服务器发送了通知。 推送服务接受了它。 订阅在数据库中。 该服务人员已注册。 四盏绿灯，零横幅。

Web 推送是一场有四个跑步者的接力赛：您的服务器签署一条消息并将其交给推送服务（Google 的，对于 Chromium 浏览器），推送服务将其保留给浏览器，浏览器唤醒服务工作者，服务工作者要求操作系统绘制横幅。 任何地方的接力棒掉落都会以同样的方式出现——终点线处一片寂静——并且没有跑步者提交报告。

第一个掉落的接力棒是我在浏览器控制台中找到的。 notification.permission 表示“默认”。 不是“授予”，不是“拒绝”——从来没有问过。 在启用通知和测试通知之间，网站的许可最终回到了原点——可能是网站数据扫描——而它授权的订阅仍然存在于我的数据库中，完全有效，指向一个不再显示任何内容的浏览器。 在这种状态下，调用 newNotification() 的页面不会出错。 它什么都不做，默默地进行，对于整个工作都受到关注的 API 来说，这是一个大胆的选择。

经过一次重新授予（这次是“永远”），横幅发挥了作用。 胜利持续了大约一个小时，直到我注意到只要浏览器不运行，推送仍然消失。

那是第二根接力棒，它隐藏在我从未质疑过的默认状态中。 推送服务很乐意为关闭的浏览器保留一条消息——这就是中继的全部意义——但前提是消息的生存时间允许。 除非另有说明，我使用的库将 TTL 设置为六十秒。 六十秒。 关闭你的笔记本电脑，两分钟后收到回复，推送服务耸耸肩并丢弃它。 它返回的 201 是完全诚实的：消息已接受。 没有人答应遵守。

修复方法是一种选择：

send_notification(payload, message, ttl: 86_400)

一天。 这大致就是“有人回答你”值得打断某人的时间；再老一点，收件箱的工作就永远不会忘记。

两堂课和这堂课一起回家了。 首先：在分层系统中，每一层都回答“OK”，错误存在于各层之间的间隙中——在授予和使用之间恢复的权限，在接受和交付之间过期的消息。 调试意味着按顺序走接力赛并询问每个跑步者“你成功了吗？” 但是“你给了下一个跑步者什么，有人接住了吗？”

第二：默认是其他人对你的产品做出的决定。 保守的浏览器权限模型、六十秒的 TTL——对于那些从未见过我的通知设置页面以及它承诺在有人回答你时听到的人来说，这都是合理的选择。 遵守这一承诺意味着找到回复和横幅之间的所有默认设置，并否决那些不同意的设置。

横幅现在到了。 即使是第二天早上。

在冰棒船上讨论这篇文章 →
